title: Interstate Commerce Simplification Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill changes the rules about when states can tax businesses from other states. Right now, states cannot tax out-of-state businesses if they only ask customers to buy things and then ship those things from outside the state. The bill makes it easier for businesses to avoid state taxes by including more business activities under the protection from taxes. This means more out-of-state businesses might not have to pay taxes to states where they sell their products.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Interstate Commerce Simplification Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Amendment
sections.text: This section changes an old law called Public Law eighty six dash two seventy two. The law gets a new definition added to it. The new definition explains what solicitation of orders means. Solicitation of orders means any business activity that helps a company ask customers to buy things. This includes activities that might also help the business in other ways besides just asking for orders.
